title: Require initialized root for file-backed operations and preserve /.agent safety
summary: Update all root-dependent runtime components so they require an initialized project root before performing filesystem, repo, verification, or artifact operations, while preserving the safety invariant that no path may resolve under '/.agent'.
acceptance_criteria: All file-backed operations require an initialized project root before they proceed.
When startup occurs with unresolved root state, root-dependent operations fail with clear guidance to run workspace_initialize(cwd) first.
No root-dependent path may resolve to '/.agent/...', including canonical, derived, and helper artifact paths.
Existing normal launches that start with a valid non-root cwd continue to work without behavior regression.

steps.description: Identify every runtime component that reads or writes repo files, .agent artifacts, .zed verification scripts, or git state through the repo context.
Replace implicit assumptions that repo_root is always available with explicit initialized-root checks at the boundaries of file-backed operations.
Ensure unresolved-root failures are actionable and consistently instruct the caller to run workspace_initialize(cwd) before retrying.
Verify that safety guards still reject any attempt to derive artifact paths from '/' and that normal non-root startup remains compatible.

notes: This ticket is about enforcement of initialized-root requirements across existing runtime code paths, not about adding the workspace_initialize(cwd) tool itself.
Prefer centralized helper checks where practical so error behavior stays consistent across repo, verify, state, and ops flows.
Do not weaken the 0.4.3 safety intent while making unresolved startup workable.
